wrapUp: Matchday sixteen closes the group stage with Spain and Uruguay meeting in Guadalajara while England assess Reece James and Declan Rice after both missed training ahead of the Panama finale on 27 June. Scotland face an anxious wait after a 3-0 defeat to Brazil in Miami, where Neymar made a tearful substitute return and Vinicius Junior scored twice. The United States topped Group D despite Kaan Ayhan's stoppage-time winner for Turkey, with Christian Pulisic fit enough to spark a late rally off the bench. Group F is settled with the Netherlands topping the section, Japan second and Sweden through as a best third-placed side, while Iran's delegation reported another airport delay before their Egypt fixture in Seattle. Each story below links to the specific report it is drawn from; squad and fitness details reflect the latest available reporting as of 26 June 2026.

sections.heading: Injuries and fitness doubts
sections.description: Players ruled out, late replacements and day-to-day concerns that could change starting line-ups and squad depth.

stories.headline: Pulisic returns off the bench as calf concern eases
stories.summary: Christian Pulisic told FOX Sports he felt "great" after a full week of training and entered the United States' Group D finale against Turkey on 26 June as a 58th-minute substitute for Timothy Weah. The co-hosts had already secured top spot and Mauricio Pochettino rested several yellow-carded regulars, but Pulisic still created several chances, hitting the post in the 72nd minute before Kaan Ayhan's stoppage-time winner gave Turkey a 3-2 consolation victory. Pulisic's availability after the calf issue that forced him off at half-time against Paraguay is a positive sign before the Round of 32 meeting with Bosnia and Herzegovina on 1 July.

stories.headline: Araujo and de Arrascaeta ruled out of Spain clash
stories.summary: Uruguay will be without Ronald Araujo and Giorgian de Arrascaeta for their Group H finale against Spain on 26 June in Guadalajara after both picked up calf problems during the group stage. Marcelo Bielsa's side need a win to guarantee progression and cannot afford to lose further creative or defensive depth against a Spain attack led by in-form Mikel Oyarzabal and Lamine Yamal, who scored on his first World Cup start against Saudi Arabia. Victor Munoz remains a minor fitness doubt for Luis de la Fuente's side, but Spain otherwise report a clean bill of health heading into a fixture that will decide the group winner.

stories.headline: Tuchel monitors James and Rice ahead of Panama finale
stories.summary: Reece James missed England's training session on 25 June after suffering a hamstring issue during the goalless draw with Ghana, throwing his fitness into doubt for the Group L decider against Panama on 27 June in New Jersey. Declan Rice, who left Boston Stadium with strapping on his left calf after playing the full 90 minutes against Ghana, was also absent from the session in Kansas City, though BBC sources indicate his situation is less concerning. Thomas Tuchel must also weigh whether to start Rice given he is one yellow card from a Round of 32 suspension, with Ezri Konsa, Nico O'Reilly and Kobbie Mainoo among the cover options if either midfielder is rested.

stories.headline: Neymar makes emotional return as Brazil beat Scotland
stories.summary: Neymar came on as a 76th-minute substitute in Brazil's 3-0 win over Scotland on 24 June in Miami, his first international appearance since October 2023 after a grade-two calf strain kept him out of the opening group matches. Vinicius Junior scored twice and Matheus Cunha added a third as Carlo Ancelotti's side secured top spot in Group C and a Round of 32 date with Japan's runners-up berth in Houston on 29 June. Neymar's cameo, greeted by chants from the crowd at Hard Rock Stadium, suggests he could play a greater role in the knockouts despite a month on the sidelines.

sections.heading: Squad selection and tactical reshuffles
sections.description: Omissions, late call-ups and manager decisions that reveal how teams plan to navigate the expanded format.

stories.headline: Pochettino rotates co-hosts but brings Pulisic off bench
stories.summary: Mauricio Pochettino rested Christian Pulisic, Chris Richards, Antonee Robinson, Tyler Adams and Folarin Balogun from the starting XI against Turkey on 26 June with Group D already won, handing Auston Trusty a rare start at centre-back. Cristian Roldan did not dress because of a muscle strain, limiting midfield cover despite the dead-rubber status of the fixture. Pulisic's second-half introduction still gave Pochettino a chance to assess his star winger's sharpness before the Round of 32, even as Turkey's Kaan Ayhan denied the co-hosts an unbeaten group campaign in stoppage time.

stories.headline: Neville urges Tuchel to start Rogers against Panama
stories.summary: Gary Neville told Sky Sports that Morgan Rogers should replace Anthony Gordon on the left wing for England's Group L finale against Panama on 27 June, arguing that Aston Villa's forward offers more invention against a deep block similar to Ghana's. Neville also backed Nico O'Reilly over Djed Spence at left-back after O'Reilly hit the crossbar in the closing stages of the 0-0 draw with Ghana. England lead the group on goal difference and a win would secure top spot before the Round of 32, though Thomas Tuchel may prioritise resting Rice or James if fitness doubts persist.

sections.heading: Disruption and preparation issues
sections.description: Travel, visa and logistical problems that could affect match readiness rather than just headlines.

stories.headline: Iran report airport delay before Egypt fixture in Seattle
stories.summary: Iran's football federation said US officials "caused problems" for Mehdi Taremi and assistant coach Saeed Alhoei on 24 June, delaying the squad's departure for Seattle by around 25 minutes ahead of their Group G finale against Egypt. The incident came days after authorities eased earlier rules that had required Iran, who are based in Tijuana, to enter the United States only shortly before matches and leave immediately afterwards. Amir Ghalenoei had criticised the cross-border arrangements as unfair and said repeated travel left his players fatigued, though Iran were permitted to arrive in Seattle two days before kick-off this time.

sections.heading: Analysis and tournament outlook
sections.description: Deeper previews on favourites, group dynamics and how the 48-team format changes the path to the final.

stories.headline: Spain and Uruguay meet with Group H top spot at stake
stories.summary: Spain can secure the Group H summit with a draw against Uruguay on 26 June in Guadalajara, having rebounded from a goalless opener against Cape Verde with a 4-0 win over Saudi Arabia driven by Lamine Yamal and Mikel Oyarzabal. Marcelo Bielsa's Uruguay sit second on two points and likely need victory to guarantee a knockout berth, with Cape Verde's result against Saudi Arabia also shaping the best-third-placed equation. The winner of Group H faces the Group J runner-up in the Round of 32, setting up a potential path that could pit Spain against tougher knockout opposition if they finish second.

stories.headline: Ayhan stoppage-time winner spoils USA unbeaten run
stories.summary: Kaan Ayhan scored in the eighth minute of added time to give Turkey a 3-2 win over the United States on 26 June at SoFi Stadium, ending the co-hosts' unbeaten start despite Auston Trusty and Sebastian Berhalter putting the USMNT ahead twice. Mauricio Pochettino's side still finished top of Group D on six points and will face Bosnia and Herzegovina in the Round of 32 on 1 July, while Turkey bow out with their only victory of the tournament. Christian Pulisic's lively cameo, including a post strike, offered encouragement even as the Americans conceded a dramatic late goal in a rotated line-up.

stories.headline: Ecuador knock Germany aside with late Plata winner
stories.summary: Ecuador beat Germany 2-1 on 25 June in New Jersey despite Julian Nagelsmann naming a strong side, with Nilson Angulo cancelling out Leroy Sane's second-minute opener before Gonzalo Plata volleyed a 77th-minute winner. The result sent Ecuador into the Round of 32 as one of the best third-placed teams, while Germany still topped Group E on goal difference despite ending an 11-match winning run. The upset also narrowed Scotland's path as a best-third qualifier, with Ecuador's four points and superior goal difference pushing Steve Clarke's side further down the ranking.

stories.headline: Netherlands top Group F as Japan and Sweden also advance
stories.summary: The Netherlands beat Tunisia 3-1 on 25 June in Kansas City to finish top of Group F on seven points, with Brian Brobbey and Jan Paul van Hecke adding to an Ellyes Skhiri own goal in a fast start that mirrored Japan's simultaneous fixture. Japan drew 1-1 with Sweden in Dallas as Daizen Maeda and Anthony Elanga traded second-half goals, leaving Hajime Moriyasu's side second and setting up a Round of 32 clash with Brazil. Sweden also advanced as a best third-placed finisher, while Tunisia exited without a point from three matches.

stories.headline: Scotland await best-third fate after Brazil hammering
stories.summary: Scotland's 3-0 defeat to Brazil on 24 June left Steve Clarke's side third in Group C on three points with a minus-three goal difference, and Clarke admitted afterwards that he thinks "we're going home." Defensive errors from Scott McKenna, Jack Hendry and Angus Gunn were punished by Vinicius Junior and Matheus Cunha, and Andy Robertson said the next few days would be "horrible" while Scotland wait to see if they qualify as one of the eight best third-placed teams. Opta models put Scotland's knockout probability at little over 5%, with favourable results elsewhere required before the group stage ends on 27 June.

stories.headline: England held by resolute Ghana in Boston stalemate
stories.summary: England dominated possession but drew 0-0 with Ghana on 23 June at Boston Stadium, with Harry Kane blazing over from close range after Nico O'Reilly hit the crossbar in the 87th minute. Thomas Tuchel rejected talk of a wake-up call and praised his side's defensive discipline, though the result extended a pattern of second-group-game stalemates at major tournaments. Both teams enter the final matchday on four points, with England facing Panama on 27 June needing a win to guarantee top spot in Group L.
